author: @Nneptune..251
text: As someone who watched the show and read the entire manga, if you think that the twins acting weird together is gross. Its too make fun of early shoujo manga. But yes it is a little weird. The twins thought that they didnt need anyone else in the world because they had thought everyone was too immature or dumb for them. Later on they join the host club and learn that its ok to have other ppl in their life. In the manga, hikaru ends up confessing that he has a crush on haruhi. Hikaru knows that haruhi is inlove with tamaki but he ends up telling her how he feels and helps haruhi get with tamaki. The twins learn how to live without always being together. Hikaru ends up dying his hair so people could look at them as different people. I love the twins, their story is so sad at times but some of their moments are weird😭

text:
His first 3 Cantonese lines were quite on point for the tone, except the last one.



EDIT: 

Since there are many ignorants, I will educate further why the dude Cantonese was so on point, accurate with the correct Vibe.

Chinese Cantonese has 9 tones vs Mandarin 4 tones. Cantonese is one of the hardest to master, even many Mandarin speakers still failed to speak with more than 20% accuracy, even when Mandarin has slight similarity to Cantonese.

I know how difficult it is because I speak fluently Chinese (Mandarin, Cantonese), English and Malay.

author: @x7heDeviLx
text: There  have been real cases where homeowners set up traps or so called booby traps to catch intruders and it almost always ends badly legally and morally. In the US, you generally cannot set up something intended to seriously injure or kill someone even if they are trespassing. Courts have consistently ruled that deadly force and deadly traps are not allowed for protecting property alone. If someone is hurt by an unlawful setup, the homeowner can absolutely be held liable in civil court and sometimes criminally as well. There have been cases where people tried it and ended up convicted or sued because the law does not treat a hidden trap the same as self defense in the moment. At the end of the day the law is pretty clear that you do not get to pre plan lethal force for property protection outside very narrow situations. Anyway still enjoying the show and that Supernatural reunion scene was great

author: @Gabe0630
text:
I’m almost done this series it’s such a good show, it’s called supernatural and is currently on prime video. Here’s a description of this particular episode with spoilers!!!! 

















Sam is already deteriorating physically and mentally after using dangerous magic connected to Rowena MacLeod. The “visions” aren’t ordinary hallucinations. They feel invasive, intimate, almost parasitic, as though Rowena’s presence has embedded itself into his mind. Throughout the episode, Sam catches glimpses of her standing silently in rooms, appearing in reflections, or speaking to him when no one else is there. Her appearances are calm and controlled, which makes them more disturbing. She never needs to scream or threaten him directly because the fear comes from the possibility that she still has influence over his thoughts.

The brothers break into the home of an elderly woman they suspect is hiding the Book of the Damned or connected artifacts. The house itself is dimly lit and claustrophobic, filled with old religious objects, heavy curtains, dust-covered furniture, and signs that someone has spent years protecting themselves from supernatural forces. There’s a constant sense that the house is less a home and more a bunker against evil.

As Sam searches through the rooms, his condition worsens. The protective magic surrounding the Book and the spells connected to it begin affecting him more intensely. He becomes disoriented, hearing Rowena’s voice whispering to him. At moments he loses track of what is real. The episode frames these visions almost like a fever dream: muffled sound design, slowed reactions, blurred focus, and sudden cuts between reality and hallucination.

Dean notices Sam slipping but doesn’t fully understand how severe it has become. There’s tension between them because Sam has been hiding how far he’s willing to go to save Dean from the Mark of Cain. That secrecy hangs over every scene. Dean sees the physical exhaustion and panic in Sam, but Sam keeps pushing forward anyway, driven by desperation and guilt.

The deeper meaning of the episode is less about the Book itself and more about obsession and sacrifice. The Book of the Damned represents forbidden hope. Everyone who touches it believes they can control its power long enough to save someone they love, but the cost is always corruption. Sam’s visions of Rowena symbolize how far he has already crossed the line. Even when she isn’t physically present, her influence remains inside him, lingering like poison.

By the end, the episode leaves the feeling that both brothers are trapped in a cycle where every attempt to save each other only pulls them further into darkness. The horror comes from realizing that neither of them knows where the moral boundary is anymore, yet neither can stop.

author: @editz-1-i4z
text:
Fudgy Brownies

Ingredients
1/2 cup (115 g) unsalted butter
1 cup (200 g) granulated sugar
2 large eggs
1 tsp vanilla extract
1/3 cup (40 g) unsweetened cocoa powder
1/2 cup (65 g) all-purpose flour
1/4 tsp salt
1/4 tsp baking powder
Optional: 1/2 cup chocolate chips or chopped nuts
Instructions
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease or line an 8×8-inch baking pan.
Melt the butter in a saucepan or microwave. Stir in sugar until smooth.
Add eggs and vanilla, mixing well until glossy.
Combine dry ingredients (cocoa, flour, salt, baking powder) in a separate bowl.
Mix dry into wet until just combined—don’t overmix.
Fold in chocolate chips or nuts if using.
Pour into pan and spread evenly.
Bake for 20–25 minutes, until the center is set but still slightly soft.
Let cool before cutting for cleaner slices.
Tips for Better Brownies
For extra fudgy texture, slightly underbake them.
For cakier brownies, add an extra egg and a bit more flour.
A pinch of espresso powder enhances the chocolate flavor (optional but great).

author: @virginiaordaya
text:
I really hope we get back to the way it was moving when I was younger. Instead of wrapping up your identity in which group you belong to it was about looking at yourself and everyone else as belonging to the same group. No matter what we look like or what our background is we are all family. We are all brothers and sisters and we all have the same Father- God. Things weren’t perfect and won’t ever be 100% perfect but it used to feel like we were all moving in the right direction. It didn’t used to be controversial for a school to have a sign that showed hands of all different skin tones saying we’re all equal. Instead the wealthy ruling class wants us divided and focusing on each other. Blaming each other for everything they’ve done to us. Fighting over resources that are only scarce because the 0.1% is hoarding them. Let’s drop all this again. Let’s break down these walls, erase these boxes, step across these imaginary lines that have no real basis in biology whatsoever. 
I miss that feeling of coming together and loving each other as one big human family under God. Forgetting who’s who’s black who’s anything other than a fellow human being. 
The only groups that actually exist in reality are the wealthy evil ruling class and the rest of us. 
Let’s remember that and come together to fight our real enemy- our common enemy. 💙💚

Lotta kids in this chat never lived in the 80s... this is a cartoon example of an 80s bully. In reality bullies in the 80s had knives and parents who barely even knew they existed. text: First one yes, counts as child abandonment. Second one, if they bought it you’re shit out of luck unless someone else bought it for you or you bought it yourself they can do whatever they want with it. Third one, very illegal and counts as fraud. While opening up a credit account on behalf of a minor isn’t itself illegal(may vary by state) using a card without the express consent of the one it is legally issued to is very much illegal. In such a scenario you can contact the company or bank it was issued from and tell them to pause the card and inform them of what’s happening, from there seek legal counsel as the law varies from state to state but you will need to compile evidence to prove you were not the one making the purchases to avoid being stuck with a debt you didn’t incur and most law firms offer free consultation on legal matters.

author: @KhảiHoàngNguyễn-z1s
text:
If a parent uses their child’s credit card without permission or opens accounts in the child’s name and creates debt, the legal consequences depend on where it happens and the specific facts. It can potentially be treated as:

* Identity theft
* Fraud
* Credit card fraud
* Financial exploitation of a minor

Penalties vary greatly by jurisdiction and by factors such as:

* How much money was involved
* Whether the conduct was repeated
* Whether the parent forged signatures or provided false information

In some places, the penalties can range from fines and restitution (repaying the money) to months or years in prison for serious fraud offenses.

If you’re asking about a real situation, tell me the country (and state/province if relevant), and I can explain the laws there. If you’re worried about debt that was created in your name, I can also explain steps to check your credit and dispute fraudulent accounts. (My uncle make me write this BTW)

author: @ROB2K88
text:
I have an autistic child who is nonverbal.  I took him to a park when he was 3, he got upset when some adults started standing in front of something he was playing with and started crying.

I brought him over to me, and I guess the way he was sitting with his body language set off the alarm for this one lady, who called the Police. It played out very similar to this scene, except for two things: 

1) I had tons of pictures on my phone of both of us
2) I'm White

There's a part of me that's grateful there's people who trust their instincts and try to protect children if they see something off.  Often, there's this power complex people have that they immediately go hostile and double down even when reasoning & evidence come into play. 

That Lady was still suspicious of me even as I skimmed through almost 4 years of photos with the cop who quickly realized I was his dad. She still thought I was pulling off some elaborate kidnapping scheme 🙄

After they engaged, i got scared and they saw howni behaved- hiding behind him, calling him daddy without being prompted, and got the picture. author: @almiller1371
text: Sleight of hand is the best magic. I was fortunate enough to meet Doug Henning one day as I was setting up his show at the Louisville Palace. He did a trick for the stagehands at break. "Who has a rolling paper?" he asked. Several hands came at him at once (go figure) and he selected a JB 1.0 from one of them. He tore it in four pieces and  began rolling the pieces up tightly as he could, until it was just a tiny ball. Then he began unrolling it, two feet away from our faces. It was perfectly intact! "You're the DEVIL!" I shouted, jokingly! He then did a rare thing and SHOWED us how he did the trick. He'd already had a JB 1.0 rolled up and stuffed under one of his long fingernails. He just swapped it with the torn one as he rolled it. Best trick I have EVER seen, and I've seen Copperfield's six-figure BS stunts many times. (He has doubles and he lies a lot!)

author: @foolishmestudios
text:
I had tingles in my arm and pain in my chest for probably about 15 year.  Just suffering until a old retired doctor working at the minute clinic for fun wacked me on both sides of my neck to figure out it was my spine all along. Wacking my neck on one side produced the symptoms. 

But isn’t that crazy how low effort it took to figure out what was wrong with me and yet 15 years of doctors, heart monitors, tests, cardiologists, etc everything constantly ruling out heart disease but never looking any further. Some doctors saying it was in my head. Others just saying they didn’t know what it could be. My parents and friends parents saying it was just in my head. 

News flash physical pain is not in your head. And even if it was, a mental illness being so bad it causes physical symptoms is pretty serious so doctors should really investigate that too

author: @BKH29
text: A quick tip I’ve learned is that if your hair is ever sticking up like when you rub a balloon on it and there’s a thunderstorm happening, you’re likely about to be struck by lightning. What you should do is crouch down and cover your ears with your hands and touch your elbows to your knees. This will give the lightning the shortest possible path to the ground, and should help reduce and damage done to your torso since it’ll flow through your arms to your legs then into the ground.

author: @SSJVegeta91
text: I'll never forget, I was 16-17 yrs old. I was walking out of my part time job at a supermarket (Jewel-Osco) in Chicago. I'm walking through the parking lot and this car pulls up to me. It was a mom and daughter, the daughter was driving. The mom said she just left the hospital but needed money for her meds. I don't remember how much she needed but I remember it basically being my weekly check (I averaged like 12hrs a week and was paid about 8/hr 😂). I will never forget how tormented the daughter looked, she never stopped looking forward, she couldn't bear to see me. That's when I realized they were telling the truth. I had just gotten paid and I gave them basically all the money I had. I hope they're doing well.

author: @SJ47668
text: A lady at a gas station approached me for gas money last year. I had just been to the bank and all I had was $100 bills - so I gave her one. My niece and her family (in the car) went nuts - I was crazy, she would drink it, all kinds of theories. I closed my ears and ignored them. I was in a position to help someone that day - I don’t often have cash on me. I didn’t give it with a proviso of what she could spend it on, and frankly, when I go out to dinner with them I’m always expected to pay and it’s never below $150 for 6-meals and drinks at chain steakhouse. I felt like I helped someone and the most it cost me was that I didn’t go to dinner with them that night - so actually saved me $50!

author: @astralsheepu7379
text:
In brazil my friend and I (her local me not) got approached by a lady on the verge of tears asking for diapers for her baby. I was there as a tourist, they weren't cheap but I could afford it. I went with her to the shop and got her some diapers of her choice and some formula. When I mentioned it once someone told me "that was definitely a scam, she probably just wanted to use the powder to cut drugs or something."

Honestly, if someone is that desperate to scam for diapers and formula, let them. I'd rather take the slim chance that they're telling the truth and a baby gets fed and cared for, and if they are using it for whatever other purposes, they're more desperate than I am for the 40 bucks. It's a blessing to be in a position where you can afford to help.
video_title: By helping others, you are also helping yourself#ChicagoFire

author: @bigkahuna3534
text: I'll never forget, I was 16-17 yrs old. I was walking out of my part time job at a supermarket (Jewel-Osco) in Chicago. I'm walking through the parking lot and this car pulls up to me. It was a mom and daughter, the daughter was driving. The mom said she just left the hospital but needed money for her meds. I don't remember how much she needed but I remember it basically being my weekly check (I averaged like 12hrs a week and was paid about 8/hr 😂). I will never forget how tormented the daughter looked, she never stopped looking forward, she couldn't bear to see me. That's when I realized they were telling the truth. I had just gotten paid and I gave them basically all the money I had. I hope they're doing wel

text:
Btw just to clear up some stuff Wild orcas are generally peaceful toward humans for humans in the Wild They do not view humans as food. While they can be curious and approach boats or divers, they typically show no aggression and usually keep their distance In Captivity they can become aggressive due to the severe stress of confinement. There have been several documented non-fatal and fatal attacks by captive orcas on trainers but The Gibraltar Incidents: Since 2020, a specific subgroup of orcas near the Strait of Gibraltar has been actively ramming and sinking boats. Scientists classify this as a localized, learned behavior (perhaps a cultural "fad" or a way for older orcas to train the young), rather than an intent to hunt or harm the humans on board

author: @NicoWill_4_life
text: As a kid I had to wear dresses to church and I felt so unsafe, like why am I only wearing underwear???? So I'd put short-shorts on underneath and my mother would tell me off if she could see them (mind you they were ugly) I just don't get these rules for even little kids, I had to take my cardigan off at school and I felt UNSAFE with the tank top I was wearing (I was like 6) I never liked the straps on bathing suites, but my grandma got it for me and said 'oh but look at that cute bunny on it! Don't you just love it!!'- umm no, but now I have to cuz I was raised to be kind. But, like everyone around me pressured me to expose myself- and that's fine for other girls, but me as a kid- not okay, I now have a really bad fear of showing any skin, no short sleeve shirts or pants- even if I'm dying of heat- I used to only wear oversized jumpers for years, and had a few heatstrokes cuz of it. I wish I wasn't told that 'this' is what I should wear- or how much of a pretty girl I was before I could say that I didn't like it, that I felt naked. (anyone else feel like this?)

author: @grindingmakeslifeeasy
text:
We had one of these in the Air Force and it was one of those perfect lineups that only happens once a decade. All the hilarious salty guys were in the same briefing at the same time. The brief was lecturing us to not be racist, or touch women and we were just so tired of being talked to like that (in the military you have these meetings every 3 months for years. Obviously, none of us had ever done that or we would have been discharged).

Once one guy responded to a scenario with "well that depends, what was she wearing?", it devolved to the most memorable 45 minutes of degeneracy I have ever experienced in the military. It was glorious, even Majors were saying some of the wildest shit you can imagine.

We were all just so tired of being talked to like we were animals.

author: @oolala53
text: I shouldn’t have been surprised, but I was the first time I heard the actor playing Cliff Clavin in real life. Neither he nor his mother sounded anything like their characters. I took Acting classes from a coach for two years who kept emphasizing the sense of spontaneity and not preparing your reactions before you played the scene, but I don’t think he could’ve played comedy one little bit. Nor created a real character. Some actors have made their way without that, people like Sean Connery or Jimmy Stewart. Or Philip Seymour Hoffman, who I admire greatly, but that still means they have to depend on a personal kind of charisma.

author: @DrunkenJonathan
text: My personal belief is that asking for all IDs depends on what you overhear and whose making the transaction. Whoever is paying needs to have ID regardless but if you overhear that it's for someone else you need to ask for their ID as well. You don't necessarily have to ID the entire group as a default (though this is entirely at the discretion of whoever is serving and specific policies). If you later see someone else drinking it then you can then ask for their ID and confiscate it if they don't have ID.

author: @helen_christie
text:
Dance marathons were one of the darkest forms of entertainment during the Great Depression. 🫣

Held across the USA throughout the 1920s and 30s, couples would dance almost nonstop weeks or months. In 1933, Callum DeVillier and Vonny Kuchinski won a marathon in Massachusetts after dancing for FIVE MONTHS STRAIGHT (3,780 hours).... taking home a $1,000 prize (roughly $24,000 today). 😱💰

Contestants were often unemployed and desperate, entering for the cash, free meals and somewhere to stay. 

Audiences paid 25 cents to watch exhausted dancers hallucinate, collapse, and mentally break down from extreme sleep deprivation (as contestants only slept in rare 15 minute windows).

After the d3ath of Homer Morehouse after 87 hours of dancing, cities like Boston, Los Angeles, Seattle, and New York began banning them. 🙅🏼‍♀️

author: @jonitabowden3494
text: I’m 60 now but I indeed drove the farm trucks sitting on phone books. My drivers ed teacher took four of us out at a time. He worked with the city kid then told the rest of us to take turns and wake him when we got back to the school. We were farm kids and had been driving for a minimum of 5 years each at that point. Some 8 years. We drove stick shift. This was a formality and he knew it. We could all change a tire and check the oil jump start and open a stuck choke. Boys and girls. Knowing wrenches and sockets and other tools by name and by sight. As drivers when our autos broke down in any way or needed maintenance we had to help our dads do the work. That’s how we learned. It has served me so well my entire life.

author: @agapitacordova3850
text:
I wasn’t driving farm equipment but cars. One of my first memories. As soon as I could see out the windshield and reach peddles I was driving. My grandfather had severe arthritis. He loved going for rides. The joint pain was to much for him so off we went. I spent the summer with them my 13th year of life. Drove my grandpa all over our small Colorado community. He was a volunteer firefighter before his disability. I would drive alone up to a burger joint for his hamburger and fries. The police would wave. 
I was responsible. I respected my elders and did as I was told. I also had a shit ton of fun driving my grandpa Ernie that summer. I’m 64 now and miss him everyday

text: I was driving my father's tractor and plowing at 6. Driving a stick shift dodge truck at 7 driving to the country store..we learn early. I'm grateful to this day for my work ethic and the money I have and still do earn ...thanks dad

author: @oilman1733
text: I’m 62 and my parents moved us from Cali to Missouri, I was in 2nd grade we spent 2.5 years there. I learned so much as a child on that dam farm it stayed w/ me all these years. Def made me a better man because of it! Gave me a better work ethic and the love of animals. I was milking cows & feeding all the animals before I even left for school. I wished we would have never came back, but I wouldn’t have met my wife and I wouldn’t have had a family and now I’m a grand father to 4. So never underestimate a child on a farm, I left one thing out my father was hospitalized a year into it, my 2 brothers & sister and I ran that lil farm for over a year on our own! First winter was the worst in 50 years 😂 we never even seen snow before but the farm doesn’t stop for bad weather, ask any farmer … this clip just brought back so great memories!
video_title: Farm Kids Grow Up Quick

author: @Epicviss
text:
Note On Footage:
Checking out a search where an agent catches a suspect lying through her teeth and decides to completely roast her for it.

The Conflict:
The suspect is acting tough and claiming she deleted all her files to hide the truth. She’s giving the agent a major attitude to try and get him to stop looking at her phone.

The Shift:
The agent finds exactly what he’s looking for almost immediately. Instead of getting into a long argument, he decides to have some fun and tricks her into walking right into a trap.

The Conclusion:
The suspect walks full speed into a "Deez Nuts" joke and has no comeback. The agent proves she was lying, gets the evidence, and gets the last laugh.

author: @zTomasTurbando
text:
When you think about it, his "rivalry" with Butcher is the only true thing in his life, the only thing that wasn't "forced" or fabricated, which is why, in this moment of "clarity" where he understands that no one will ever love him or respect him without fear, he is so joyful to see someone who has a GENUINE feeling about him, even if it's hatred.

Every person that ever "loved" Homelander was either forced by him or others, or fabricated that love for gains. Every person that respected him faked it to survive or to push an agenda like Stormfront did. Even Homelander's public personality was, for most of the time, a fake to try and get the attention and the love and the affection and the respect.

Butcher's hatred however is sincere. It's the only sincere thing in Homelander's life. It's why Homelander was so mesmerized when he scanned Butcher at the beggining of the season and realized his hatred was so real and pure that Butcher had mutated himself into the thing he hates the most (supes) solely to be able to get revenge on him. Homelander wants to be the center of everybody's world, and the only person who actually has him as the center of their world is Butcher.

Dance does everything a team sport does - except it is on "streiods" as you can be the quarterback / running back and defensive line player all in one shot.
